person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
geni.com
Raoul VI de Toeni (de Tosny), Lord Flamstead
French: de Toéni, Lord Flamstead
Also Known As:	"Raoul", "de Toni", "de Toeni", "de Tosny"
Birthdate:	between circa 1189 and 1191
Birthplace:	Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England
Death:	September 29, 1239 (47-54)
at sea 
Place of Burial:	St. Giles in the Woods Priory Flamstead Hertfordshire, England 
Immediate Family:	
Son of 
Roger IV (V) de Tosny, Lord of Flamstead, Knight de Conches and 
Constance de Beaumont
Husband of Petronilla de Lacy

Father of 
Constance de Tosny; 
Roger de Toeni, V and 
Alice de Tosny
Brother of Richard de Toeni;
 Joan de Toeni; Roger de Toeni; 
Marguerite de Tosny; 
John de Toeni; 
Robert de Tosny / Toni / Toeni and 
Constance de Tosny / Toni / Toeni 

Occupation:	Lord of Flamstead, Crusader

RALPH IV DE TOENI, 1st son and heir, was born probably in 1189 or 1190. In 1204, with his father and brother or brothers, he was excluded by the King of France from the terms of the pacification in Normandy. King John ordered the manors of Saham and Ryhcot to be restored to him, 5 November 1213; and he was with the King at Partenay, in Poitou, 26 May 1214. Presumably he supported John in the civil war, for on 7 April 1216 the King granted him the lands which had been held by Richard de Montfichet in Essex, Bucks, Cambridge, Norfolk, Suffolk and Hunts; but soon afterwards he must have joined the rebellious barons, for John ordered the sheriff of co. Worcester, 16 July 1216, to give Robert de Mortimer seisin of the land of Abberley, held by Ralph, and on 5 September following he gave the manor of Flamstead to Waleran Tyes. By Henry III Ralph was granted the manor of Newport, Essex, during pleasure, 27 June 1218. Shortly before 20 September 1233 he was given the custody of Maud Castle (Painscastle, co. Radnor); and later in that year he and John de Monmouth were appointed generals of the Poitevin mercenaries in the Welsh marches against the Earl of Pembroke and Llewellyn. 

On 11 March 1233/4 he was ordered to keep the truce with Llewellyn until 25 July; with other nobles he was forbidden, 2 September 1234, to go to tournaments arranged at Northampton, Cambridge or elsewhere; and he was summoned, 7 March 1237/8, with other barons of the Welsh marches, to be at Oxford after Easter to confer with the King. In 1239 he took the Cross and set out for the Holy Land. He is said to have founded a monastery in the west of England. 4997

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ralph de Tosny於1190年出生在英國賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德時，他的父親Roger de Tosny34歲，母親Constance de Beaumont30歲。他於1233年9月與Petronilla de Lacy在英國英格蘭爾拉德諾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1239年9月29日在英國英格蘭賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德享年49歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀特福爾達可盧姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tosny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
Americanized form of any of various surnames ultimately derived from the Latin personal name Antonius (see Anthony ), for example Greek Antoniou and Italian Toni .
French: from the personal name Tony, a pet form of Antonin (from Latin Antoninus, a derivative of Antonius; see Anthony ).

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph de Tosny was born in 1190, in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, his father, Roger de Tosny, was 34 and his mother, Constance de Beaumont, was 30. He married Petronilla de Lacy in September 1233, in Radnor, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 29 September 1239, in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49, and was buried in Dacorum, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ralph de Tosny est né en 1190, à Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger de Tosny, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Constance de Beaumont, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Petronilla de Lacy en septembre 1233, à Radnor, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 29 septembre 1239, à Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Dacorum, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ralph de Tosny 1190 in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger de Tosny 34 und seine Mutter Constance de Beaumont 30. Er hat im September 1233 in Radnor, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Petronilla de Lacy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 29. September 1239 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Dacorum, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph de Tosny è nato il nel 1190 nel Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, il suo padre, Roger de Tosny, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Constance de Beaumont, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Petronilla de Lacy nel settembre 1233, nel Radnor, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 29 settembre 1239, nel Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Dacorum, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Ralph de Tosnyが1190年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドで生まれた時，父Roger de Tosnyは34歳，母Constance de Beaumontは30歳であった。1233年9月にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアラドノーでPetronilla de Lacyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1239年9月29日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドで49歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアダコルムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph de Tosny(이)가 1190년 영국 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger de Tosny(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance de Beaumont(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1233년 9월 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 라드너에서 Petronilla de Lacy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1239년 9월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 다코럼에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph de Tosny nasceu em 1190, em Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger de Tosny, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Constance de Beaumont, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Petronilla de Lacy em setembro de 1233, em Radnor, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 29 de setembro de 1239, em Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 49 anos, e foi sepultado em Dacorum, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ralph de Tosny родился в 1190 г. в местности флэмстид, хиртфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger de Tosny было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Constance de Beaumont, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Petronilla de Lacy сентябрь 1233 г. в местности раднор, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 29 сентября 1239 г. в местности флэмстид, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности дакорум, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ralph de Tosny nació en 1190, en Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger de Tosny, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Constance de Beaumont, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Petronilla de Lacy en septiembre de 1233, en Radnor, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 29 de septiembre de 1239, en Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultado en Dacorum, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
